name: MITRE ATT&CK T1059.001 — Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
description: Adversaries use PowerShell for execution, discovery, lateral movement, and download cradles. Key detection: Event 4104 Script Block Logging, Sysmon Event ID 1 with -EncodedCommand or -enc, unusual parent-child process chains (cmd.exe → powershell.exe).

name: Sysmon Configuration and Event Reference Guide
citation: Microsoft Sysinternals Sysmon v15.x Event Reference
description: Sysmon provides granular endpoint telemetry. Critical event IDs: 1 (ProcessCreate — Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, IntegrityLevel, Hashes), 3 (NetworkConnect — SourceIp, DestinationIp, DestinationPort), 7 (ImageLoad — DLL hijacking), 11 (FileCreate), 22 (DNSQuery). Primary LOLBin detection field: CommandLine in Event ID 1.

name: Windows Security Event ID 4625 — Account Failed to Log On
citation: Microsoft Docs: Windows Security Audit Events, Event ID 4625
description: Event 4625 fires on the target host when logon fails. Key fields: TargetUserName, LogonType (3=network), IpAddress, Status/SubStatus (0xC000006A=wrong password, 0xC0000234=locked). Pattern: single IP targeting many accounts with same password = password spray; many IPs one account = credential stuffing.

name: Microsoft Sentinel KQL Hunting Query Library
citation: Microsoft Sentinel GitHub Community: Hunting Queries
description: Curated KQL hunting queries for Sentinel. Key tables: SecurityEvent (Windows events via AMA), DeviceProcessEvents and DeviceNetworkEvents (Defender XDR endpoint), SigninLogs (Entra ID), OfficeActivity. Common operators: summarize, make-series, series_stats, project, extend, join.

name: NIST SP 800-61r2 — Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
description: Standard IR lifecycle: Preparation → Detection & Analysis → Containment, Eradication & Recovery → Post-Incident Activity. §3.3.2 mandates volatile evidence collection first (RAM, running processes, network connections) before disk imaging. Containment: short-term (isolate, disable account) vs. long-term (patch, reimage).

name: Lateral Movement Detection via Sysmon and Windows Events
citation: Threat Hunting via Sysmon — SANS SOC Summit 2023
description: PsExec: services.exe spawning psexesvc.exe then cmd.exe (Sysmon ID 1). WMI: wmiprvse.exe spawning unexpected children. Pass-the-Hash: Event 4624 LogonType=3 + NtlmV1. SMB admin share access: Event 5140 ShareName=ADMIN$ or C$. Correlate source and target auth events.

name: Microsoft Defender XDR Advanced Hunting Table Schemas
citation: Microsoft Docs: Advanced Hunting Schema Reference
description: Core Defender XDR KQL table schemas: DeviceProcessEvents (DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, SHA256), DeviceNetworkEvents (DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl, Protocol, InitiatingProcessFileName), DeviceLogonEvents (AccountName, LogonType, RemoteIP), AlertInfo (Title, Severity, AttackTechniques).

name: C2 Beacon Detection Using JA3 TLS Fingerprints
citation: John Althouse et al., JA3: A Method for Profiling SSL/TLS Client Hello Messages, 2019
description: JA3 is an MD5 fingerprint of TLS Client Hello parameters (SSLVersion, Ciphers, Extensions, EllipticCurves). Known-bad JA3 hashes identify C2 frameworks. Hunt: group flows by JA3, flag identical rare hashes reaching rotating external IPs from multiple internal hosts, correlate with process tree.

name: Windows Security Event ID 4769 — Kerberos Service Ticket Requested
citation: Microsoft Docs: Windows Security Audit Events, Event ID 4769
description: Event 4769 fires when a Kerberos TGS is requested. Kerberoasting pattern: one user account requesting TGS for many service accounts using TicketEncryptionType=0x17 (RC4-HMAC, weak) in a short window. Normal baseline: AES256 (0x12). MITRE T1558.003.

name: MITRE ATT&CK T1543.003 — Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
description: Adversaries create or modify Windows services for persistence and execution. Detection: Event 4697 (service installed), System Event 7045 (new service), Sysmon ID 1 for sc.exe children. Suspicious: services running from temp directories, single-char service names, ImagePath using rundll32 or regsvr32.
